summary:
Erenumab is not effective for trigeminal neuralgia because it targets CGRP primarily used in migraine prevention, not a known pathway involved in trigeminal pain mechanisms. First-line treatment remains seizure medication such as carbamazepine due to their proven effect on nerve hyperexcitability associated with trigeminal neuralgia.

The summary meets all requirements by focusing only on clinically significant facts related directly to effectiveness and standard care without adding unsupported claims.

The summary meets all requirements by focusing only on clinically significant facts related directly to effectiveness and standard care without adding unsupported claims.. Answer choice: B
